text: ni ame̱kē̱: Ofai ne̱, wo̱nuṅtšo̱! Kleṅkleṅ ní wo̱ba niyenii hemo̱ le̱,
translated: My child: Come here, you are blessed! Whatever you touch turns to gold.

ref: Isaac was old and almost blind, when he called in his first-born son Esau, who asked him, “Father, what can I do for you?”
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni Isak egbo̱, ni ebĩṅme̱ii ano̱ eha ni enaa nii dšogbāṅ le̱, etšé̱ ebi nukpa Esau le̱, ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Mibi! Ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Nami!
translated: And he said, bring me some of the game, my son, so that I may eat and my soul may bless you. And he brought it to him, and he ate; and he brought him wine, and he drank. And his father Isaac said to him, come near and kiss me, my son. And he came near and kissed him.

ref: About that time one of the king's daughters came down to take a bath in the river, while her servant women walked along the river bank. She saw the basket in the tall grass and sent one of them to pull it out of the water.
text: Ni Farao biyō le̱ ba ebedšū ye̱ fā le̱ nā, ni eweyei le̱ mīšĕra ši ye̱ fā le̱ toi; ni ena adeka le̱ ye̱ gălãi le̱ amli, ni etšũ eweyō yawó ke̱-ba.
translated: Pharaoh was not the one who gave you the well, nor did he dig it for you; it was the Lord who gave you the well, and He also provided the water.

translated: I am going to the farm today, I will return in the evening. My friend will accompany me.

ref: Now please tell me if you are willing to do the right thing for my master. Will you treat him fairly, or do I have to look for another young woman?
text: Ni agbē̱ne̱, ke̱dši nyeke̱ minuṅtšo̱ aaye ye̱ mlihìle̱ ke̱ ano̱kwayeli mli le̱, no le̱ nyekē̱ mi; ni ke̱-dšee nakai hũ, nyekē̱ami, koni mawo ninedšũrõ lo abe̱kuiaṅ tšṍmo̱.
translated: I am telling you, if you do not have love in your heart for your neighbor, you have nothing; even if you have all other things, you have nothing, because love is the greatest of all virtues.

ref: God blessed Isaac after this, and Isaac moved to a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.” Ishmael's Descendants
text: Ni Abraham gbẽle se̱ le̱ Nyoṅmo̱ dšō̱ ebi Isak; ni Isak hĩ ši ye̱ hĩekãlo̱ ni naämi le̱ *nubu le̱ hewo̱. *
translated: Abraham gave all he had to Isaac. But while he was still living, he gave gifts to the sons of his concubines and sent them away from his son Isaac to the land of the east.

translated: When Joseph's brothers saw that their father loved him more than all his brothers, they hated him and could not speak peacefully to him.

ref: Now hurry back and tell my father that his son Joseph says, “God has made me ruler of Egypt. Come here as quickly as you can.
text: Nyebe afea oyá ni nyeyaä mitše̱ ṅõ, ni nyekē̱ale̱ ake̱: Bo̱ni obi Iosef kē̱ ne̱: Nyoṅmo̱ ewomi Mizraim fẽ no̱ nuṅtšo̱; ba miṅõ, kātše̱!
translated: In the time of the old year, when the corn was ripe, and the harvest was near, they said to Joseph: Behold, your father is in the land of Egypt and is sick; come quickly!

ref: and so they told the king of Egypt to set the people of Israel free. The Lord Commands Moses and Aaron To Speak to the King
text: Ame̱dši mei ní ke̱ Farao, Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱, wie, koni adšie Israelbii le̱ ke̱-dše̱ Mizraim le̱; nakai Mose ke̱ Aaron le̱ ne̱.
translated: Then the king of Egypt said to them, "Moses and Aaron, why do you distract the Israelites from their work? Go to your labors!"

ref: They spent a long time in prison, and the official in charge of the palace guard, made Joseph their servant.
text: Ni oblafoi atše̱ le̱ tuame̱ ewo Iosef deṅ, ni esṅmoo̱ame̱, ni ame̱tše̱ kplaṅ ye̱ tšũ le̱ṅ.
translated: When Joseph's brothers saw him coming, they were afraid, and they planned to kill him.

ref: Now listen carefully and do what I say. Go to the home of my brother Laban in Haran
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ agbe̱ne̱, mibi, bo migbē toi, ni ote ši ni odšo foi ke̱-ya minyemi Laban ṅõ ye̱ *Haran, *
translated: Now, the sons that Bethuel begot were these: Rebekah, and her brother, Laban. And Bethuel was the son of Milcah, the wife of Nahor, Abraham's brother. And Rebekah bore these to Isaac: Jacob and Esau. And Esau took wives from the daughters of the Canaanites: Adah, the daughter of Elon the Hittite, and Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah, the daughter of Zibeon the Hivite, and Basemath, the daughter of Ishmael, the sister of Nebaioth. And Jacob hearkened to his father and his mother and went to Paddan-aram, to Laban, the son of Bethuel the Aramean, the brother of Rebekah, the mother of Jacob and Esau. And Esau saw that the daughters of Canaan did not please his father Isaac. So Esau went to Ishmael and took Mahalath, the daughter of Ishmael, Abraham's son, the sister of Nebaioth, to be his wife, besides the wives he had. And Jacob went out from Beersheba and went toward Haran.

text: Ni ekē̱: Mẽ awaba matoõ? Ni ekē̱: Owolonāso̱omo̱nõ ke̱ ehe kpã ke̱ odẽhĩemo̱tšo le̱. Ni eṅõ-hãle̱, ní eba eṅõ, ni eke̱le̱ ná mũsũ.
translated: I said: What is the news? He said: The elders and the chief are having a meeting. I waited a while, then I went, and I found them sitting.

ref: The Lord has been good to my master and has made him very rich. He has given him many sheep, goats, cattle, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of silver and gold, and many slaves.
text: Ni Iehowa edšō̱ minuṅtšo̱ naakpa, ni etšṍ gbomo agbo; ni ehãle̱ tōi ke̱ tšinai, ke̱ dſie̱te̱i ke̱ šika, ke̱ wehĩĩ ke̱ weyei, ke̱ yomai ke̱ tedšii.
translated: I am Jehovah, the one teaching you to benefit yourself, the one guiding you in the way you should walk. I will give you insight and instruct you in the way you should go; I will give counsel with my eye upon you.

ref: Then God continued: I know you are innocent. That's why I kept you from sleeping with Sarah and doing anything wrong.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ kē̱le̱ ye̱ lamõ le̱ mli ake̱: Mi hũ mile ake̱ otšui mū oke̱-fe ene̱; ni mi hũ mitšĩ onā, ní okafe eša ošimi; nohewo̱ ni mihāão hegbe̱ ake̱ ota ehe le̱.
translated: This is what the Lord God says: I am going to take the Israelites out of the nations where they have gone. I will gather them from all around and bring them back into their own land.

ref: Abram was very rich. He owned many cattle, sheep, and goats, and had a lot of silver and gold.
text: Ni Abram ená nii babaō ye̱ šĩa koloi ke̱ dſie̱te̱i ke̱ šika gbe̱fãṅ. Abe̱i
translated: Abram was the one who first made a car and used it to go to the farm.

ref: God named the dry ground “Land,” and he named the water “Ocean.” God looked at what he had done and saw that it was good.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ tšé̱ heni egbĩ le̱ šikpoṅ, ni nui le̱ anābuamo̱ le̱ etšé̱le̱ ṅšo̱. Ni Nyoṅmo̱ na ake̱ ehĩ.
translated: I am Nyonmo̱, I came from the sky, I have come to bring blessings and good things. I am Nyonmo̱, I have arrived.

translated: Oh Canaanite merchants, your eyes have been opened by your money!

ref: Laban also gave Zilpah to Leah as her servant woman.
text: Ni Laban ṅõ eweyō Zilpa ehã ebi Lea, ni efe eweyõ.
translated: Laban gave his daughter Leah to Jacob, and also gave her his servant Zilpah.

ref: The next morning Jacob found out that he had married Leah, and he asked Laban, “Why did you do this to me? Didn't I work to get Rachel? Why did you trick me?”
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni dše tše̱re le̱, na, Lea ni. Ni ekē̱ Laban ake̱: Meni ofemi ne̱? Ani dšee Rahel hewo̱ ni misúmo̱o le̱? Mẽhewo̱ mo̱ ni ošišiumi ne̱?
translated: I asked my mother, "Why are you doing this to me? Wasn't I serving you for Rachel? So why have you deceived me?"

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: Ni edšō̱le̱ ni ekē̱: Aadšō̱ Abram ahã ṅwei flōflo Nyoṅmo̱ ni dši ṅwei ke̱ šikpoṅ tše̱ le̱;
translated: And God said to Abram: Leave your country, your relatives, and your father's house, and go to the land that I will show you;
